Informal statement: For any finite `(T, A)` distribution `Q`, if the state type is at least as large as the action type, there are two finite PMFs with the same observable `(T, A)` marginal: one with `I(S; A | T) = 0`, and one with `I(S; A | T) = H(A | T)`.
Paper claim supported: The finite-discrete core of the output-trace identifiability gap / Theorem 1 narrative.
Assumptions: Finite types, decidable equality, `Fintype.card A <= Fintype.card S`.
Not claimed: No claim about real-world identifiability, continuous states, or how an auditor learns which PMF generated a deployment.

Informal statement: If a probe `X` reaches action `A` only through state `S` conditional on trace `T`, then `I(X; A | T) <= I(S; A | T)`.
Paper claim supported: Proposition 2 / conditional-DPI dynamic certificate.
Assumptions: Finite PMF over `(Probe, State, Action, Trace)` and concrete `condMarkov` predicate.
Not claimed: Does not prove that an arbitrary empirical probe is admissible; the conditional Markov premise must be justified separately.

Informal statement: Lifting a `(State, Action, Trace)` PMF with a deterministic readout `probe : State -> Trace -> Probe` automatically satisfies the concrete `condMarkov` predicate.
Paper claim supported: Markov-premise discharge for conservative dynamic probes.
Assumptions: Finite PMF over `(State, Action, Trace)` and deterministic probe readout from `(State, Trace)`.
Not claimed: Does not cover probes that read the action, extra hidden variables, or randomized probe noise not included in `(State, Trace)`.
Depends on: `deterministicProbePMF_apply`, marginal identities for the lifted PMF.

Informal statement: For a deterministic readout of `(State, Trace)`, `I(Probe; Action | Trace) <= I(State; Action | Trace)` without an external `condMarkov` premise.
Paper claim supported: Conservative dynamic lower-bound entry point with the Markov premise discharged by construction.
Assumptions: Finite PMF over `(State, Action, Trace)` and deterministic probe readout from `(State, Trace)`.
Not claimed: Does not certify arbitrary empirical or randomized probes unless they are represented as deterministic readouts of the modeled state and trace.

Informal statement: The maximum over replay, intervention, and proxy lower terms remains bounded by the corresponding maximum of true dynamic relevance terms.
Paper claim supported: Aggregating several valid dynamic probe classes.
Assumptions: Each probe distribution satisfies `condMarkov`.
Not claimed: Does not compare probes from different audit abstractions unless their PMFs and traces are already aligned by the caller.

Informal statement: The visible/full trace entropy gap decomposes exactly as `H(S | T_tilde) = H(S | T_full) + I(S; M | T_tilde)`.
Paper claim supported: Trace-gap identity used in the structural budget proof.
Assumptions: Finite PMF over `(State, VisibleTrace, MissingTrace)`.
Not claimed: Does not itself bound `I(S; M | T_tilde)`; it is only the algebraic decomposition.

Informal statement: If an external cut bound gives `I(S; M | T_tilde) <= C_cut(Omega)`, then `H(S | T_tilde) <= H(S | T_full) + C_cut(Omega)`.
Paper claim supported: Proposition 1 / structural cut-set upper budget.
Assumptions: Finite PMF and explicit caller-supplied cut/information-flow bound.
Not claimed: Does not prove the network cut-set inequality by itself; the cut bound is an explicit premise.

Informal statement: The static state-entropy gap is bounded by the entropy, and therefore log-cardinality, of the missing trace.
Paper claim supported: Coarse finite-support sanity bound for structural hidden trace entropy.
Assumptions: Finite missing-trace type.
Not claimed: Not a replacement for the structural cut-set budget; it ignores graph topology and edge capacities.

Informal statement: Under software orthogonality, the static cut bound can be replaced by an additive edge-sum bound.
Paper claim supported: Additive min-cut structural budget corollary.
Assumptions: Finite PMF
Not claimed: cut-bound premise
column11: Does not prove software orthogonality from program source code or runtime traces.

Informal statement: After pushing `(S, T, M)` to `(S, Cut, M, T)`, conditional DPI bounds `I(S; M | T_tilde)` by `I(Cut; M | T_tilde)`.
Paper claim supported: Formal cut-set DPI step in the structural budget chain.
Assumptions: Finite cut variables and `condMarkov (pmf_from_vars P Omega_vars)`.
Not claimed: Does not prove the cut variable was correctly extracted from a real deployment graph.

Informal statement: Combining the cut-set DPI step with a capacity bound yields `I(S; M | T_tilde)` by `C_cut`.
Paper claim supported: Bridge from topology/DPI to a numeric cut capacity.
Assumptions: `condMarkov` for the pushed PMF and capacity premise `I(Cut; M | T_tilde) <= C_cut`.
Not claimed: Does not derive the numeric capacity from interface specs.

Informal statement: The extracted cut-set premises imply the final structural state-entropy upper bound.
Paper claim supported: Convenient end-to-end Lean entry point for Proposition 1 from cut variables.
Assumptions: Finite PMF, cut-variable map, `condMarkov`, and capacity upper bound.
Not claimed: Does not verify graph extraction, capacity estimation, or logging instrumentation.

Informal statement: For finite PMFs, `condMarkov` implies `I(X; Z | W) <= I(Y; Z | W)`.
Paper claim supported: Conditional data-processing inequality used by both the dynamic and cut-set certificate routes.
Assumptions: Finite four-variable PMF and concrete algebraic `condMarkov`.
Not claimed: Does not assert `condMarkov` holds for a deployment; it proves the inequality once the premise is supplied.
Depends on: Chain-rule identities and nonnegativity of conditional mutual information.

Informal statement: A finite Markov chain `A -> B -> C` has `I(A; C | B) = 0`.
Paper claim supported: Algebraic zero-CMI bridge for three-variable d-separation examples.
Assumptions: Finite PMF and concrete `IsMarkovChain` factorization.
Not claimed: Does not prove a DAG globally Markov factorizes; it consumes the Markov-chain premise.

Informal statement: For a positive finite Markov model, graph d-separation plus local-Markov and graphoid assumptions imply the corresponding conditional-independence equation on finite assignments.
Paper claim supported: Typed graph-to-statistical bridge.
Assumptions: Caller supplies a `PositiveMarkovModel`, plus `DSeparationQuery` and `dSeparates` for that query.
Not claimed: Not the full Verma-Pearl global Markov theorem; core graph-to-CI bridge assumptions are resolved via MarkovGenerator -> GlobalMarkov -> UnsafeBridge.

Informal statement: For tuple labels `0 = X`, `1 = Y`, `2 = Z`, `3 = W`, a positive Markov model plus `{0} _||_ {2} | {1,3}` implies concrete `condMarkov` on the projected PMF.
Paper claim supported: DAG-to-DPI bridge used in four-variable cut-set and dynamic certificate chains.
Assumptions: Finite typed DAG model, `DSeparationQuery`, and `dSeparates` for `{0} _||_ {2} | {1,3}`.
Not claimed: Does not synthesize the d-separation proof or the Markov assumptions automatically.

Informal statement: For labels `0 = X`, `2 = Y`, `1 = Z`, a positive Markov model plus `{0} _||_ {2} | {1}` implies a concrete three-variable Markov-chain equation on the projection.
Paper claim supported: Three-variable d-separation to Markov-chain bridge.
Assumptions: Finite typed DAG model, `DSeparationQuery` for `{0} _||_ {2} | {1}`, and `dSeparates`.
Not claimed: Does not prove global Markov semantics from raw graph syntax alone.

Informal statement: In the three-variable layout, positive Markov assumptions and d-separation imply `I(A; C | B) = 0`.
Paper claim supported: First-arrow bridge from d-separation to CMI-zero claims in the typed 3-tuple setting.
Assumptions: Finite PMF from a positive Markov model, `DSeparationQuery`, and `dSeparates`.
Not claimed: Does not close the general probabilistic graphical-model theorem.

Informal statement: On the pairwise-disjoint query domain, separation in the moralized ancestral graph implies every trail from `X` to `Y` is blocked by `Z`.
Paper claim supported: Soundness/completeness direction connecting the graph criterion to trail blocking for valid d-separation queries.
Assumptions: `X`, `Y`, `Z` are pairwise disjoint.
Not claimed: The unrestricted statement is false; endpoints in `Z` are excluded by the query-domain premise.

Informal statement: The same graph-to-trail d-separation result specialized to the `DSeparationQuery` predicate.
Paper claim supported: Public query-domain wrapper for d-separation completeness.
Assumptions: `DSeparationQuery X Y Z`.
Not claimed: Does not cover malformed queries where endpoints are conditioned on.

Informal statement: The unrestricted implication `DAG.dSeparated G X Y Z -> dSeparates G X Y Z` is false.
Paper claim supported: Guards the paper/artifact against overclaiming d-separation outside the standard disjoint query domain.
Assumptions: Uses an explicit finite counterexample.
Not claimed: Not a negative result about standard d-separation; it only rejects the malformed unrestricted statement.

Informal statement: If the operative state is deterministically recoverable from the trace, no exact endogenous internal-state witness can have residual same-trace variation.
Paper claim supported: Autoregressive exact-core / zero-cut corollary.
Assumptions: Deterministic screen `Trace -> State` and exact equality `S omega = recon (T omega)`.
Not claimed: Does not formalize positive-epsilon entropy or CMI conditions; it uses exact finite same-trace surrogates.

Informal statement: If the operative state is deterministically recoverable from the trace, no endogenous finite-Shannon EIS witness can have `0 < H(I | T)`.
Paper claim supported: Paper-facing autoregressive EIS impossibility for residual autonomy.
Assumptions: Finite outcome PMF; finite `I`, `A`, and `Trace`; deterministic screen `S = recon (T omega)`; endogeneity `I = chi(S)`.
Not claimed: Exact deterministic theorem only; does not prove quantitative entropy-budget or epsilon-screenability bounds; decision relevance is in the witness but unused by the contradiction.

Informal statement: Reader-facing alias for the exact trace-recoverability impossibility theorem.
Paper claim supported: Paper-table entry for deterministic trace recoverability.
Assumptions: Same as `no_eis_autoregressive`.
Not claimed: Adds no stronger theorem beyond the alias.

Informal statement: If full state is predictable from trace at error `eps`, then no projection `I = chi(S)` can satisfy the legacy unpredictability-above-`eps_min` surrogate when `eps < eps_min`.
Paper claim supported: Legacy off-root predictability compatibility theorem; not paper-facing.
Assumptions: Minimal monotone `ProbSpace`, predictability premise, and `eps < eps_min`.
Not claimed: Not an entropy theorem: it does not prove `H(I | T) > 0` impossibility, sigma-additive `ProbSpace`, causal influence, or CMI decision relevance.

Informal statement: No finite-query certifier can be both sound and complete for the global closedness property under the stated stability condition.
Paper claim supported: Finite-query impossibility core.
Assumptions: Certifier output depends only on a finite queried support
Not claimed: encoded by `FiniteQueryCertifier.stable`.
Depends on: Does not rule out randomized, distributional, interactive, or restricted-domain auditors not satisfying the formal certifier interface.

Informal statement: A reward/function respects semantic equivalence exactly iff it factors through the quotient by that equivalence.
Paper claim supported: Semantic closure as quotient factorization.
Assumptions: A setoid on the example/state type.
Not claimed: Does not construct the semantic equivalence relation for a real dataset or model.

Informal statement: A finite support cannot `rho`-cover an infinite `gamma`-separated sequence when `rho < gamma / 2`.
Paper claim supported: Geometric non-covering / separated-packing impossibility.
Assumptions: Metric representation space and `IsGammaSeparatedInjection`.
Not claimed: Does not prove that a learned representation has such a separated sequence; that is a modeling premise.

Informal statement: If seen examples `rho`-cover a set and rewards are Lipschitz in normalized representation distance, each covered point has a seen witness with `FCG <= L * rho`.
Paper claim supported: Covering-to-format-channel-gap bound.
Assumptions: `IsRhoCover`, Lipschitz reward premise, normalized representation distance, and `L >= 0`.
Not claimed: Does not estimate representation distance or prove empirical cover quality from data.

Informal statement: If a high-probability region is covered, then the good-FCG set has probability at least `1 - eps`.
Paper claim supported: High-probability version of the covering certificate.
Assumptions: Measurable good-FCG set
Not claimed: probability measure
column12: Does not show measurability or high-probability coverage from data.

Informal statement: Given the paper statistical derivation for the explicit Fano and missed-cell terms, the combined PAC lower bound is their maximum.
Paper claim supported: Paper-facing Theorem 3 wrapper for the PAC lower-bound algebraic core with concrete terms from `provenance/fano_bound.md`
Assumptions: `PACPaperStatisticalDerivation K m alpha tau sigma epsilon delta`.
Not claimed: Does not formalize the Gaussian observation experiment, KL/Fano theorem, conditional missed-cell measure argument, or estimator-to-decoder probability lifting; those remain the paper proof.

Informal statement: The spike reward family is pairwise separated at scale `alpha * tau` under the finite spike distance.
Paper claim supported: Packing-family separation step in the PAC argument.
Assumptions: `0 <= alpha * tau`.
Not claimed: Does not model Gaussian observations or sampling.

Informal statement: If a learner succeeds within `epsilon < sep / 4` in a separated family, nearest-neighbor decoding recovers the true index.
Paper claim supported: Reduction from PAC reward learning success to hidden index recovery.
Assumptions: Pairwise separation
Not claimed: triangle interface
column12: Does not lower-bound sample complexity by itself.

Informal statement: A KKT-style certificate with per-symbol information bounded by `C` proves `I(Y; Z | W) <= C`.
Paper claim supported: Capacity-certificate pipeline for cut-set bounds.
Assumptions: Valid `KKT_Certificate` fields: weighted decomposition
Not claimed: per-symbol bounds
column12: Does not construct the KKT certificate for arbitrary channels.

Informal statement: A `d`-dimensional `Q`-ary quantized vector has entropy at most `d log₂ Q`.
Paper claim supported: Interface-budget calculation for finite-resolution vector channels.
Assumptions: `0 < d`, `0 < Q`, finite PMF on `Fin (Q ^ d)`.
Not claimed: Does not justify the quantization scheme or its empirical error.

Informal statement: In the two-state example, the cut-set route proves `I(S; M | T_tilde) <= 1` from a concrete `condMarkov` premise.
Paper claim supported: Minimal typed deployment sanity check for the structural certificate stack.
Assumptions: Two-valued cut variable, finite PMF, and concrete `condMarkov`.
Not claimed: Not a general deployment theorem; it is a small case study.

Informal statement: The one-bit case-study bound follows from the DAG automation interface when positive model and d-separation assumptions are supplied.
Paper claim supported: End-to-end example connecting d-separation
Assumptions: Markov bridge
Not claimed: KKT capacity
column11: `PositiveMarkovModel`, d-separation for `{0} _||_ {2} | {1,3}`, and matching projected cut-variable model.
column12: Does not derive the positive model nor the `project4PMF` equality from raw graph syntax.

Informal statement: The legacy name for `linear_chain_cut_set_bound_of_dSeparated`.
Paper claim supported: Compatibility shim for historical references.
Assumptions: `PositiveMarkovModel`, d-separation for `{0} _||_ {2} | {1,3}`, and matching projected cut-variable model.
Not claimed: Does not derive the positive model nor the `project4PMF` equality from raw graph syntax.

Informal statement: Historical bridge name specialized to the typed positive-model API.
Paper claim supported: Compatibility wrapper for the current d-separation to CI bridge.
Assumptions: `PositiveMarkovModel`, `DSeparationQuery`, node containment, and `dSeparates`.
Not claimed: Does not provide a closed bridge from raw DAG syntax to generic finite-model conditional independence; it delegates to `dsep_implies_CI`.,`dsep_implies_CI`."
